companies.name: TCS
companies.overview: TCS is India's largest IT services company, hiring freshers primarily through TCS NQT (National Qualifier Test) and TCS Ninja/Digital drives. Interviews focus on aptitude, programming fundamentals, and communication skills.
tips: Revise core CS subjects: OOPs, DBMS, OS, and Networking basics
Be ready to explain your resume projects in depth
Practice communication in English as TCS weighs soft skills heavily
Stay updated on TCS's recent business verticals and client work

companies.name: Infosys
companies.overview: Infosys hires freshers mainly via InfyTQ and off-campus drives for the Systems Engineer and Digital Specialist Engineer roles. Interviews emphasize logical reasoning, pseudocode, and puzzle-solving.
tips: Practice pseudocode-based logical questions, a signature Infosys pattern
Brush up on basic puzzles and case-study questions
Know the difference between Systems Engineer and Specialist Engineer tracks
Keep your project explanations simple and outcome-focused

companies.name: Cognizant
companies.overview: Cognizant's GenC (Generation Cognizant) program is the primary fresher hiring pipeline. The process tests coding fundamentals, aptitude, and communication through AMCAT-style assessments.
tips: Practice AMCAT-pattern quantitative and logical questions
Be thorough with at least one programming language end-to-end
Prepare a 60-second self-introduction
Research GenC Elevate vs GenC Next tracks before applying

companies.name: Accenture
companies.overview: Accenture hires Associate Software Engineers through a cognitive assessment, coding round, and a combined technical-HR interview known for its conversational, scenario-based style.
tips: Practice situational and scenario-based HR questions
Be comfortable discussing consulting-style client scenarios
Revise SQL and OOP concepts thoroughly
Show adaptability and teamwork examples from college projects

companies.name: Wipro
companies.overview: Wipro's WILP and Elite NTH (National Talent Hunt) drives assess candidates on aptitude, essay writing, coding, and a technical-cum-HR interview.
tips: Practice timed essay writing on current tech topics
Revise basic data structures and SQL joins
Be clear about your preferred domain (support/testing/development)
Prepare for questions on relocation flexibility
